name: Jump-Scare Trigger
description: Jump-Scare Trigger is a fear-building mechanic that shapes vulnerability, uncertainty, attention, and tension.
design_notes: Jump-Scare Trigger should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For horror mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use jump-scare trigger as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect jump-scare trigger to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels vulnerable, alert, and uncertain about what happens next.

parameters.name: tension_level
parameters.description: Current pressure or fear intensity used to drive the mechanic.

parameters.name: resource_drain
parameters.description: Rate at which a safety or visibility resource is consumed.

parameters.name: reveal_distance
parameters.description: Distance at which a threat or cue becomes clear.

edge_cases: Jump-Scare Trigger is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Jump-Scare Trigger overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Jump-Scare Trigger runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Jump-Scare Trigger state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Jump-Scare Trigger plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Jump-Scare Trigger produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Jump-Scare Trigger strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Jump-Scare Trigger becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Jump-Scare Trigger with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Jump-Scare Trigger as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Jump-Scare Trigger with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Jump-Scare Trigger as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
